cure.ref: Marianski — meatsandsausages.com Cure Calculator / Curing for sausages
cure.note: Cure #1 (6.25% NaNO2): 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite, the standard for cooked-smoked sausage. Current cure=1 g/kg ≈ 62 ppm — under-cured; raise to 2.5.

safe.note: Min safe 68°C (154°F); US gov/FSIS recommends 71°C (160°F). App ground-meat floor displays 71°C. Cook to internal 68–71°C.

process.note: Stepped: dry 45–55°C (no/light smoke) 10–40 min → smoke 45–60°C 30–90 min → cook 80–85°C (or poach 80°C) until internal 68–71°C.

flag: cure was 1 g/kg (~62 ppm, under-cured) → recommend 2.5
rationale: Emulsified cured hot-smoked sausage. Bumped cure 1→2.5 g/kg (156 ppm standard); salt 18 fine; internal 71°C per ground-meat floor.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite (standard). Current cure=1 ≈ 62 ppm — under-cured; raise to 2.5.

safe.note: Safe 68°C, gov 71°C; app displays 71°C ground-meat floor. Cook to 68–71°C internal.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 45–60°C 30–90 min → cook 80–85°C or poach 75–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

flag: cure was 1 g/kg (~62 ppm, under-cured) → recommend 2.5
rationale: Emulsified cured hot-smoked frank. Cure raised 1→2.5; salt 18 kept; 71°C floor cited.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite. Current cure=1 ≈ 62 ppm — under-cured; raise to 2.5.

safe.note: Emulsified cured sausage; internal 68–71°C. App displays 71°C ground-meat floor.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → light smoke 45–60°C → cook 80–85°C (or steam/poach) until internal 68–71°C.

flag: cure was 1 g/kg (~62 ppm, under-cured) → recommend 2.5
rationale: Cured emulsified sausage cooked in smoker. Cure 1→2.5; salt 18 kept; 71°C floor.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite. Current cure=None — a cured Portuguese sausage held warm in the smoker needs nitrite; add 2.5.

safe.note: Coarse cured hot-smoked sausage; cook to 68–71°C. App displays 71°C ground-meat floor.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → heavy smoke 50–60°C 60–120 min → cook 80–85°C until internal 68–71°C.

flag: cure was None → smoked cured sausage needs nitrite; recommend 2.5
rationale: Portuguese hot-smoked sausage; added missing cure (None→2.5) and salt (None→20 g/kg); 71°C floor.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite. Current cure=None — held warm in smoker; add nitrite at 2.5.

safe.note: Greek coarse cured smoked sausage; cook to 68–71°C. App 71°C ground-meat floor.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 45–60°C → cook 80–85°C to internal 68–71°C.

flag: cure was None → smoked sausage needs nitrite; recommend 2.5
rationale: Greek hot-smoked sausage; added cure None→2.5 and salt None→18; 71°C floor.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite. Current cure=None — long warm smoke on thin sticks strongly needs nitrite; add 2.5.

safe.note: Thin cured smoked sticks; cook to 68–71°C. App 71°C ground-meat floor.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 50–65°C 1–3 h → cook 75–80°C until internal 68–71°C.

flag: cure was None → smoked sausage needs nitrite; recommend 2.5
rationale: Thin snack sticks spend hours warm in the smoker; added cure None→2.5 and salt None→20; 71°C floor.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm nitrite. Current 2.5 is correct — no change.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → heavy smoke 55–70°C several hours → cook to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Cure 2.5 and salt 18 already standard; documented 71°C floor and stepped hot-smoke.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 45–60°C → cook 78–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Smoked bockwurst; cure/salt already standard; documented safety and process.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 45–60°C → cook 78–82°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Emulsified cooked-smoked cervelat; cure/salt standard; documented.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → heavy smoke 50–60°C → cook 80–85°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Portuguese coarse smoked sausage; cure 2.5/salt 20 standard; documented.

process.ref: Marianski — Hot smoking / Polish smoked kielbasa
process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 50–60°C 90–120 min → cook 75–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Polish country smoked kielbasa; cure/salt standard; documented Marianski schedule.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 50–60°C → cook 80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Polish cooked-smoked krakowska; cure 2.5/salt 20 standard; documented.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 50–60°C → cook 78–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Slovenian Carniolan smoked sausage; cure/salt standard; documented.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → heavy smoke 50–60°C → cook 80–85°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Brazilian/Portuguese calabresa-style smoked sausage; cure 2.5/salt 20 standard; documented.

cure.note: Cure #1: 2.5 g/kg = 156 ppm. Current 2.5 correct (semi-dry fermented-then-cooked; Cure #1 appropriate for cook-and-hold).

safe.note: Cook to 68–71°C internal (semi-dry summer sausage cooked in smoker). App 71°C ground-meat floor.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 50–65°C → gradually raise smoker to 75–80°C until internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Semi-dry cooked-smoked summer sausage; cure 2.5/salt 20 standard; documented.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → light smoke 45–60°C → cook/poach 75–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Emulsified veal/pork bockwurst; cure/salt standard; documented.

safe.note: Cook to 68–71°C internal. App 71°C ground-meat floor.

process.note: Rind-rich Italian sausage traditionally poached; if smoked: dry 45–55°C → light smoke → cook 80°C to internal 68–71°C (long simmer to soften rind).

rationale: Italian rind sausage; cure 2.5/salt 22 (higher for large diameter) standard; documented cook-to 71°C.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → paprika-smoke 50–60°C → cook 78–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Hungarian paprika smoked sausage; cure/salt standard; documented.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 50–60°C → cook 75–80°C to internal 68–71°C (keep below cheese-melt scorch).

rationale: Cheese-filled Carniolan sausage; cure/salt standard; documented, cook gently to 71°C.

process.note: Dry 45–55°C → smoke 45–60°C → cook/poach 75–80°C to internal 68–71°C.

rationale: Emulsified garlic knackwurst; cure/salt standard; documented.
